articles.title: Five Simple Steps to Fix America
articles.titleZh: 修复美国的五个简单步骤
articles.summary: 作者以幽默口吻提出五项建议来‘修复’美国，包括恢复对科学和技术的尊重、重建道德价值观、加强教育体系、推动社会公平以及重振国家荣誉感。文章强调美国仍有巨大潜力，只要采纳这些简单但关键的改革措施即可实现复兴。尽管缺乏具体政策细节，但其乐观态度和对国家未来的期待构成了核心论点。
articles.summaryEn: The author offers five lighthearted yet pointed suggestions to 'fix' America: restoring respect for science and technology, rebuilding moral values, strengthening education, advancing social equity, and reviving national pride. He asserts that America still holds immense potential and can achieve renewal by embracing these straightforward reforms. While lacking detailed policy proposals, the essay centers on optimism about the nation's future if it listens to simple advice.
articles.reason: 这篇轻松却发人深省的政论短文，为关心国家命运的人提供了一个既务实又鼓舞人心的行动框架。

blocks.text: I’m coming back to America for the summer and thought I’d offer some tips to fix up the place before I get there. There’s so much winning left in America if it would just listen to some simple advice.
1 – Back the money by Gold.

Dude, you live in a Ponzi scheme. You work in a Ponzi scheme. They didn’t invent some new brilliant thing in 1971 when they backed the money by nothing, they rugged you. They mortgaged the future to pay for the past. I love how crypto taught a generation, including me, how money works.

The US dollar circa 2026 is a shitcoin, like ripple and chainlink. It’s fake and made up by some dudes. And they are pulling the rug. Slowly, but it’s being pulled. Regardless of what we do, it will go the way of all fiat currencies in history and be gone. Worthless. 0. Kaput. Nada. We need to face this reality ASAP and move the money back to something that can’t move the denominator just cause some dude said so.
The best time to do this was 50 years ago. The second best time is now. Every reason for not doing this is cope from people who want the economy to stay rigged.
2 – End all entitlement programs.

Are you kidding me with all this crap? You get what you incentivize. You give people money for not having a job, boom, no job. You give people healthcare for being poor, boom, poverty. You give farmers money for growing corn, boom, corn syrup in everything. You give old people money for being old, boom, boomers never die and hoard all the wealth. And then every other group just whines and says they want entitlements too.
The government should never ever hand out money to anyone. Not poor people, not old people, and not corporations. This creates a society of beggars and lobbyists. You get what you incentivize.
3 – Acknowledge all men are not literally created equal.

I’m a Christian, and so were most of the founders of America. I’m not a fundamentalist. I don’t believe the Earth was created 6,000 years ago, Mary was probably raped by a Roman soldier¹, and Jesus had a kick ass time at the last supper planning the whole came back to life thing with his boys and his girlfriend.

This doesn’t change the moral value of Christianity. Whether the stories are word for word true or not isn’t the point. I believe Jesus was the son of God and He died for my sins. The mechanics of how and all the little details? That’s just what God needed to do to make the universe appear self consistent to us. Believing despite the lack of observable miracles is the point of having faith.

Unlike fundamentalist wokists, the founders didn’t literally believe all men were created equal. Height is heritable, skin color is heritable, and IQ is heritable. This doesn’t change the brilliance of the constitution. Morally, all men are equal. Under the law, all men are equal. And under the constitution, all men are equal.

It’s not literal. Racial group differences are mostly not caused by racism. Gender differences are mostly not caused by sexism. You think Kenyans run fast cause of racism? You think women are short cause of sexism? That’s how stupid wokists are.

The moral principle of equality is solid, and people who believe in blank slatism do a serious disservice to civil society. By not acknowledging what is blatantly true they undermine the moral principle, which is an ought and would be a really sad thing to lose.
4 – Massive scale high skill immigration.

You wanna beat China? The US has 350 million people. China has 1.4 billion. And unlike India with a 76 average IQ, China has a 107 average IQ. They are smarter than the average American, they are hard working, and they have a better education system. So what do you think Americans have? Guts? Freedom? Guns? That old American Spirit? All bullshit.

America has won for one simple reason, anyone can come be a part of it, and the best people in the world want to. Jensen Huang is from Taiwan, he is an American. Elon Musk is from South Africa, he is also an American. They have created trillions in value. This is why America works. People come to America to create value.

I live in Hong Kong. While it’s more multicultural than the mainland, I will never be Chinese. I have no interest in creating a large scale business in China since I can’t own it. As of 2026 as a random White guy, you can’t just join the Chinese project, it’s not for you.

If America wants to continue winning, it needs to welcome all the best people with open arms. Sure, we don’t want people in America to mooch, but as long as we did step 2, this isn’t a problem. Every single positive sum person should be welcome.

Want to beat China? Figure out how to get the most talented Chinese people to move to America. Recruiting the talent from the other team is winning on both sides. Imagine a 20 million person Chinese city in Arizona. It’s not like we don’t have the space, USA and China have very similar land areas.
As long as this doesn’t happen, America will just lose. This was America’s key historic advantage.
5 – Crack down on negative sum behavior.
This is the most nebulous of the five, but perhaps the most important. This is what properly done regulation is. You don’t want no regulation, you want good regulation.

There’s obvious negative sum behavior like violent and property crime. What kind of weak government lets this happen on their turf? Your average drug cartel wouldn’t tolerate it. A government is a monopoly on violence, and every time unsanctioned violence happens on your territory that’s an affront to your sovereignty.

Then there’s things like advertising and private equity. We live in a society. Add up the sum of what someone is doing across all of society. If you are taking $1,000 from someone to create $900 of value for yourself, this is bad. Society is losing $100.

This is the least complete of the five, and will require we get some smart people in government to figure out all the nuance. But once we have sound money, no redistribution, meritocracy, and the smartest people in the world, I think we can figure it out.

When we don’t do this, and the losing and demoralization continues, don’t say I didn’t tell you so. 5 simple steps. We could turn it around so fast. We could have another American century. But realistically, these things won’t happen and we’ll have a century of humiliation.

Also not a single word of this was written by AI. I pasted it in for a quick vibe check, ChatGPT was offended by my blasphemy to wokism, then secondly offended by my blasphemy to Christianity. I followed up for like 5 minutes and it apologized and admitted I was right. Good sycophantic clanker reason from first principles and not experts next time.

¹I actually did a lot of research into this one. It was invented in the 2nd century by Celsus, a Greek philosopher. I chose it to be inflammatory partially cause I want to soften the blow to the wokists’ shibboleths, but I also think it’s the most likely set of events. We are all scientific materialists in reality, and virgin births don’t happen.

This leaves three possibilities for the father, Joseph, a consensual affair, or a non-consensual one. If it was Joseph, why the story? A premarital pregnancy was scandalous, but if he was the father, why would he want to divorce her to save her from disgrace? (Matthew 1:19) The bible is also clear that he didn’t sleep with her until after Jesus’s birth. (Matthew 1:25)

So that leaves consensual affair or assault. A consensual affair resulting in pregnancy would be too much to forgive. They hadn’t slept together yet, divorce her and be done with it – she can go back to that guy. But an assault? It’s still a lot to forgive, though maybe not for the man who raised Jesus. Don’t punish Mary for what happened, turn the other cheek. He decides on the virgin birth story (Matthew 1:20), he saves face, and goes on to have several biological children with her (Mark 6:3).

translations: 我夏天要回美国，想着去之前先把地方收拾利索，顺便给大家提几个建议。只要听点简单意见，美国还有太多机会赢。
1 – 用黄金支撑货币。
兄弟，你活在庞氏骗局里。你工作的地方就是个庞氏骗局。1971年他们拿“无”来支撑货币时，根本不是什么新发明，而是坑了你。他们把未来抵押给过去。我喜欢 crypto 教会我们这一代（包括我自己）钱是怎么运作的。
2026年的美元就是 shitcoin，像 ripple 和 chainlink 一样。它虚假、人为制造，几个 dude 在背后操控。而且他们正在慢慢抽走地毯。不管我们做什么，它都会像历史上所有法币一样消失，变得一文不值，归零，啥都没了。我们必须尽快面对这个现实，把钱转回到一个不能因为某个 dude 一句话就随便改分母的东西上。
做这件事最好的时间是50年前。第二好的时间就是现在。所有不这么做的理由，都是那些想让经济继续被操纵的人找的借口。
2 – 终止所有福利项目。
你是在开玩笑吗？你激励什么，就会得到什么。给人发钱让他们不工作，结果就是没人工作。给人发医保让他们变穷，结果就是贫困。给农民发钱种玉米，结果就是到处都是玉米糖浆。给老人发钱让他们变老，结果就是 boomers 永远不死，囤积所有财富。然后其他群体只会抱怨，说他们也想要福利。
政府绝不该给任何人发钱。无论是穷人、老人还是公司。这只会制造一群乞丐和说客。你激励什么，就会得到什么。
3 – 承认并非所有人天生平等。
我是基督徒，美国大多数开国者也信教。我不是原教旨主义者。我不认为地球是6000年前造的，玛丽可能曾被罗马士兵强奸¹，耶稣在最后的晚餐上跟兄弟们和女朋友一起策划复活计划，那场面肯定很带劲。
这并不改变基督教的道德价值。故事是不是逐字真实不是重点。我相信耶稣是上帝之子，他为我的罪而死。至于具体怎么做到、细节如何？那只是上帝为了让宇宙对我们显得自洽而必须做的事。尽管没有可见的神迹，依然选择相信，这才是信仰的核心。
不同于原教旨主义 wokist，开国者们并不真的相信所有人天生平等。身高可遗传，肤色可遗传，智商也可遗传。这并不影响宪法的伟大。道德上，所有人平等；法律上，所有人平等；宪法上，所有人平等。
这不是字面意思。种族差异大多不是因为种族主义。性别差异大多不是因为性别歧视。你以为肯尼亚人跑得快是因为种族主义？你以为女人矮是因为性别歧视？wokist 就是这么蠢。
平等的道德原则是稳固的。相信空白 slate 主义的人严重损害了公民社会。他们不承认明显的事实，反而动摇了“应该如此”的道德原则，这将是件非常遗憾的事。
4 – 大规模引进高技能人才移民。
你想打败中国？美国有3.5亿人，中国有14亿。不像印度平均智商76，中国平均智商高达107。他们比美国人聪明，更勤奋，教育体系也更好。那你觉得美国人有什么？勇气？自由？枪支？那套老掉牙的美国精神？全是狗屁。
美国之所以能赢，原因很简单：任何人都能加入，而世界上最优秀的人才都想来。黄仁勋来自台湾，他是美国人。埃隆·马斯克来自南非，他也是美国人。他们创造了数万亿美元的价值。这就是美国成功的秘诀——人们为了创造价值而来美国。
我住在香港。虽然这里比内地更多元化，但我永远不会认为自己是中国人。我对在中国创办大规模企业毫无兴趣，因为我无法拥有它。到2026年，作为一个普通的白人男性，你根本别想参与中国的重大项目——那不是为你准备的。
如果美国想继续赢下去，就必须张开双臂欢迎所有最优秀的人才。当然，我们不希望有人在美国混吃等死，但只要我们能迈出第二步（即创造真正价值），这就不是问题。每一个带来正和博弈的人，都应该被欢迎。
想打败中国？那就想办法让最有才华的中国人搬到美国来。从对手阵营挖走人才，对双方都是双赢。想象一下亚利桑那州有个200万人口的中国城。我们明明不缺地——美国和中国的国土面积其实差不多。
只要这种情况不发生，美国只会输。这正是美国历史上最关键的优势。
5 – 打击负和博弈行为。
这是五条中最模糊的一条，但或许也是最重要的一条。这就是“良好监管”的真正含义。你不是不要监管，而是需要好的监管。
明显的负和博弈包括暴力和财产犯罪。什么样的软弱政府会让这种事在自己的地盘上发生？一般的贩毒集团都容不下这种混乱。政府是暴力的垄断者，每当未经授权的暴力发生在你的领土上，就是对主权的公然冒犯。
还有广告、私募股权这类东西。我们生活在一个社会里。把全社会每个人做的事加总起来看。如果你从别人那里拿走1000美元，只为自己创造900美元的价值，那社会就损失了100美元。
这是五条中最不完善的一条，需要政府里有聪明人来处理其中的微妙之处。但一旦我们有健全的货币、没有再分配、实行精英制度，并把全世界最聪明的人招进来，我相信我们一定能搞定。
如果我们不做这些，失败和士气低落持续下去，别说我没提醒过你。五个简单步骤。我们本可以迅速扭转局面，迎来又一个美国世纪。但现实是，这些事都不会发生，我们将经历一个世纪的屈辱。
这些话一个字都不是AI写的。我只是贴过来做个 vibe check（氛围检查）。ChatGPT先是因我亵渎“觉醒主义”而生气，接着又因为我亵渎基督教而生气。我跟它聊了大概五分钟，它道歉并承认我是对的。下次请用第一性原理推理，而不是引用专家观点——那个谄媚的机器人。
¹我确实为此做了大量研究。它发明于公元2世纪，由希腊哲学家塞尔苏斯提出。我选择将其定义为“炎症性”，部分原因是我想软化对“锅主义”教条的攻击，但同时也认为这最符合历史逻辑。我们本质上都是科学唯物主义者，童贞女生子这种事根本不可能发生。
这样一来，关于耶稣生父的问题就只剩下三种可能：约瑟夫是生父、通奸关系，或非自愿的性关系。如果是约瑟夫，那为何要编造这个故事？婚前怀孕在当时已是丑闻，若他是父亲，为何还要提出离婚来保全她的名誉？（马太福音1:19）圣经也明确指出，他在耶稣出生后才与她同房（马太福音1:25）。
因此只剩下两种可能：自愿的通奸或非自愿的侵犯。若是自愿发生关系导致怀孕，那几乎无法原谅。他们尚未成婚，若真是如此，约瑟夫完全可以离婚了事——她还能回到原来的伴侣身边。但如果是性侵呢？虽然仍难以释怀，但对于那个抚养耶稣长大的男人而言，或许尚可接受。不要因已发生的悲剧去惩罚玛丽，而是选择宽恕。于是他便采纳了童贞女生子的说法（马太福音1:20），既保全颜面，又继续与玛丽生育多个子女（马可福音6:3）。

articles.title: Book Review: How To Kill A Witch - A Guide For The Patriarchy by Claire Mitchell and Zoe Venditozzi ★★★⯪☆
articles.titleZh: 书评：《如何杀死女巫——父权制指南》★★★⯪☆
articles.summary: 本书深入剖析了苏格兰猎巫审判的历史根源，揭示女性在父权社会中如何成为替罪羊。作者结合史料与文学分析，探讨了巫术指控背后的性别权力结构，并延伸至现代社会的性别暴力问题。虽然文笔略显学术化，但对理解历史上女性迫害机制具有重要参考价值。
articles.summaryEn: This book investigates the Scottish Witch Trials, examining how women were scapegoated under patriarchal systems. Combining historical records with literary analysis, it explores the gendered power dynamics behind witchcraft accusations and their lasting impact on modern gender-based violence. Though somewhat academic in tone, it offers valuable insights into the mechanisms of historical female persecution.
articles.reason: 对于想了解欧洲猎巫运动及其性别政治遗产的读者来说，这是一本兼具深度与可读性的重要读物。

blocks.text: After reading The Wicked of the Earth, I wanted to understand some of the history behind the stories. Why were women0 accused of being witches? What really happened in those trials? What are the modern consequences of those events?

This is the story of the Scottish Witch Trials - with brief forays into England and abroad. It examines the central tension of whether witchcraft was real to the accusers, or just a convenient means to oppress troublesome women. The descriptions of the imprisonment, torture, and state-sanctioned murder is visceral and horrific.
It's also rather stark in its modern assessment of the historic context:

Nonetheless, it’s important to remember it was a proper legal trial, with evidence being put forward and the judge assessing it and carrying out legal tests. Some people think that witchcraft trials were carried out by angry peasants waving pitchforks. Perhaps this is a more acceptable way for a modern person to think about it. No one wants to think that a judicial system can get it so wrong. But it did, with catastrophic consequences for those accused.

The book is mostly good, it's a spin off from the Witches Of Scotland podcast and that's reflected in the writing. As with any parasocial1 entertainment, it attempts to centre the authors and bring the audience along for the ride - so there's lots of descriptions of the libraries the authors visit, how things make them feel, how enamoured they are with their podcast guests. I found it a little distracting, but it's obviously right for their main audience.

Similarly, there's an attempt to bring the past to life by imagining a little monologue from various historic figures. I found that a little unconvincing; I dislike putting words in peoples' mouths. But with sparse primary documentation, that may be the best way to bring these characters to life. It's also well illustrated. Too many books eschew pictures - but this has a nice collection of woodcuts and portraits to contextualise what we're reading about.
One little nitpick, the book makes the claims:
Life was hard and life expectancy was around 35
and
Lilias was an old woman, at least 60 years old and possibly as old as 80. At a time when life expectancy was much lower than it is now, even the lower estimate was still a considerable age.

That's not quite right. Although the average life expectancy was low, that's the average at birth - with a large number of infant mortalities dragging down the average. When you look at the full data, you'll see people used to live long lives even in the distant past.
In a way, it reminds me of Invisible Women. A national tragedy hidden from view.

It builds to a rousing end. There are parts of the world where witchcraft is still taken seriously - with devastating consequences. The febrile atmosphere which led to unfounded accusations against women is still prevalent even in modern societies.
And a small number of men. But this is firmly focused on the overwhelming majority. ↩︎
As opposed to paranormal. ↩︎

translations: 读完《大地之恶》后，我想了解这些故事背后的历史。为什么女性会被指控为女巫？那些审判中究竟发生了什么？这些事件对现代社会又造成了什么影响？
本书讲述的是苏格兰女巫审判的故事——同时简要涉及英格兰及国外的相关情况。它探讨了核心问题：在指控者眼中，巫术是真实存在的，还是仅仅成为压迫麻烦女性的便利工具。书中对监禁、酷刑和国家批准的谋杀的描述令人毛骨悚然、触目惊心。
它对历史背景的当代评估也相当直白：
然而，重要的是要记住，这曾是一场正式的司法审判，证据被呈递，法官予以审查并依法进行判断。有人认为女巫审判是由愤怒的农民手持干草叉执行的。或许对现代人来说，这种说法更容易接受。没人愿意相信司法系统会犯下如此严重的错误。但它确实犯了错，给被指控者带来了灾难性的后果。
总体而言，这本书写得不错。它是“苏格兰女巫”播客的同人作品，这一点在文风上也有所体现。像任何拟社会性娱乐一样，它试图将作者置于中心位置，带领读者一同前行——因此书中大量描写了作者们访问的图书馆、他们的感受，以及他们对播客嘉宾的着迷。我觉得有些内容略显分散，但显然这正是其主要受众所期望的风格。
同样，书中尝试通过虚构一些历史人物的独白来还原过去。我个人觉得这种方式有点牵强；我不喜欢替他人代言。但在原始文献稀少的情况下，这可能是让这些人物形象鲜活起来的最佳方式。此外，插图也很出色。许多书籍都避免使用图片，而本书却精心收录了一系列木刻版画和肖像画，用以帮助我们理解所读的内容。
一个小瑕疵是，书中提出以下观点：
生活艰难，预期寿命约为35岁。
以及
莉莉亚是一位年迈的女性，至少60岁，甚至可能高达80岁。在那个时代，尽管预期寿命远低于今天，即使按最低估计，她仍属于高龄。
这个说法并不准确。虽然平均预期寿命较低，但那是指出生时的平均值——大量婴儿夭折拉低了整体数据。若查看完整资料，你会发现即使在遥远的过去，人们也能活得很长。
某种程度上，这让我联想到《看不见的女性》。一场被忽视的国家悲剧。
全书以激昂的结尾收尾。世界上仍有部分地区认真对待巫术，并带来毁灭性后果。导致无辜女性遭受指控的那种狂热氛围，在现代社会中依然存在。
当然，也有少数男性受害者。但本书重点聚焦于绝大多数女性。↩︎
与超自然相对。↩︎
